rulings.Case Title: ANA VEGA VS. OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Service Of Summons Due To Lack Of Jurisdiction; Or In The Alternative Request For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action For Inconvient Forum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 28, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT OUTBACK STEAKHOUSE OF FLORIDA, OSI RESTAURANT PARTNERS Motion for Order Quashing Service of Summons Due to Lack of Jurisdiction; or in the Alternative Request for Dismissal or Stay of Action for Inconvenient Forum. Hearing continued to December 9, 2009 to allow limited discovery regarding minimum contacts. Mihlon vs. Superior Court (1985) 169 Cal.App. 3d 703. No competent evidence presented that defendants are subject to jurisdiction in California. A foreign corporation's designation of an agent for service of process in California is not a submission to personal jurisdiction here absent "minimum contact" with California. Gray Line Tours of Southern Nevada vs. Reynolds Electric & Engineering Co., Inc. (1987) 193 Cal.App.3d 190. Control of local subsidiary does not, without more, subject a corporation to jurisdiction in California. DVI, Inc. vs. Superior Court (Papworth) (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 1080. Supplemental opposition due November 24. Supplemental reply due December 4. =(302/CWW)
